title: thymus serpyllum subsp. pulcherrimus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. pulcherrimus, commonly known as the wild thyme or creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading, evergreen shrub with tiny pink or white flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum subsp. pulcherrimus also contribute to its numerous health benefits, including reducing stress, improving respiratory function, and boosting the immune system. Thymus serpyllum subsp. pulcherrimus can be prepared as teas, infusions, tinctures, or essential oils, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to colds and flu.
